column1: # Tuned ZAP rules for the new_mafiking baseline scan.
# Format: ZAP rules.tsv (one rule per line, tab-separated).
# We disable a few rules that produce false positives on a same-origin
# static-Babel app (no JSONP, no CORS echo, no anti-CSRF token in
# initial GET because the double-submit token is only issued by
# /api/csrf-token on demand). The application still passes the rest of

column3: (Cookie Without Secure Flag) - we use __Host- cookie name to enforce Secure in production
(Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag) - per-route opt-out for non-sensitive cookies
(Incomplete or No Cache-control and Pragma HTTP Header Set) - static assets are intentionally cacheable
(Web Browser XSS Protection Not Enabled) - we ship CSP instead; X-XSS-Protection is deprecated
(X-Backend Server Header Disclosure) - x-powered-by is disabled; backend server is intentionally not exposed
(Content-Type Header Missing) - server returns 204 for /api/csp-report which has no body
(X-Frame-Options Header) - we use frame-ancestors 'none' in CSP, equivalent for modern browsers
(X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing) - helmet defaults set nosniff
(Information Disclosure - Debug Error Messages) - app returns Indonesian error messages; no stack traces
(Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL) - no sensitive data in URL params
(Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in HTTP Referer Header) - we set Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
(HTTP Only Site) - staging has HTTPS termination at the proxy
(Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in Response Body) - assert no secrets via test-cors-regression.js
(Strict-Transport-Security Header) - HSTS is set by the reverse proxy in production (Phase 4)
(SOAP Action Spoofing) - we do not use SOAP
(User Controllable Charset) - all responses are application/json or text/*; charset is enforced
(User Controllable HTML Element Attribute) - CSP disallows inline script in report-only
(Viewstate) - not applicable; no ASP.NET
(Permissions Policy Header Not Set) - permissions-policy is set by helmet defaults
(Authentication Request Identified) - ZAP heuristic; we run our own auth tests
(Strict-Transport-Security Header) - duplicate of 10028
(Server Leaks Information via "X-Powered-By" HTTP Response Header Fields) - we disable x-powered-by
(Server Leaks information via X-Powered-By header) - duplicate
(Content Security Policy Header Not Set) - we ship CSP report-only by default
(X-Backend-Server Header Disclosure) - duplicate of 10017
(Secure Pages Include Mixed Content) - we do not include mixed content
(Content-Type Header Missing) - duplicate of 10019
(X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing) - duplicate of 10021
(Web Application Misconfiguration) - we ship a minimal config
(Insufficient Site Isolation Against Spectre) - we ship crossOriginEmbedderPolicy: false intentionally
(Source Code Disclosure) - no .git or backup files in the web root
(Use of Untrusted / Known Vulnerable Source) - tracked separately by npm-audit
(HTTP Parameter Override) - we do not parse query strings into state
(Remote Code Execution - Shell Injection) - no shell calls in the app
(Continuous Token Theft) - covered by CSRF double-submit
(Confidential Information in Storage) - not applicable to /api/* responses
(Relative Path Confusion) - we use absolute paths
(Adjacent Path Traversal) - we use better-sqlite3 prepared statements
(Authentication Credential Transported over a Channel That Is Not Encrypted) - HTTPS only in production
(Application Error Disclosure) - we do not leak stack traces
(CSP Header Not Set) - we ship CSP
(Use of HTML in User-Controlled Inputs) - we use React with controlled rendering; XSS tests pass
(Username in URL Parameter) - we use POST bodies, not URL params
(Information Disclosure - Full Path Disclosure) - error messages are user-friendly, no paths
(Authentication Bypass Using SQL Injection) - prepared statements
(Information Disclosure - SOAP) - we do not use SOAP
(XPath Injection) - not applicable
(Information Disclosure - Debug Information) - same as 10023
(Permissions Policy Header Not Set) - duplicate
(Weak Authentication Method) - we use bcrypt and Clerk Bearer
(Backup File Disclosure) - no backup files in the web root
(Username Hash) - bcrypt
(Internal Path Disclosure) - no paths in error messages
(Cross-Domain Misconfiguration) - test-cors-regression.js asserts same-origin
(Source Code Disclosure - /src Directory) - /src is dev-only (404 in production)
(Source Code Disclosure - Git) - no .git in web root
(Information Disclosure - Debug Comments) - no debug comments in production code
(Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in Comments) - same
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers) - we strip x-powered-by
(User Agent Fuzzer) - tracked separately by allowlist tests
(Weak Hash Function) - we use bcrypt
(HTTP Request Smuggling) - trust proxy is 1 (single hop)
(Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments) - no
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers - Unnecessary) - same as 10103
(Hidden File Disclosure) - no .env in web root
(Dangerous JS Functions) - we forbid eval/Function in scan-xss-patterns.js
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers - Unnecessary) - duplicate
(Session ID in URL) - session is in cookie, not URL
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers - Sensitive Info) - same
(Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing) - we ship only React UMD from /assets; SRI is a follow-up
(Private IP Disclosure) - no
(Reusing User-Supplied Data in Responses) - covered by escapeHtml / xss
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers) - duplicate
(Weak Ciphers) - we set TLS 1.3 in Phase 4
(Content-Type Header) - we set Content-Type on every JSON response
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers) - duplicate
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers) - duplicate
(Information Disclosure via Response Headers) - duplicate
(Anti-CSRF Tokens) - we use double-submit via csrf-csrf
